translations.translation:
Dong Xi (Yuandai)
董襲 (元代)
Lived AD ?–217
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 55 (Wu 10)
byZL181
onFebruary 27th, 2025

Dong Xi, styledYuandai, was fromYuyao[餘姚],Kuaiji[會稽]. He was eightchitall1and his martial strength exceededothers.A•••
Assuming onechiwas 24 centimeters at the time,Dong Xiwould have been 6.3 feet, or 192 centimeters, making him tall among his peers. “Eightchi” could also not be literal, and instead be a metaphor for how tall he was.↩
TheBook of Later Hanby Xie Cheng acclaimed[Dong] Xiof being sincere and fervent in his ambitions and integrity, and that his martial ability and resolve was fierce and powerful.
WhenSun Ceentered the commandery,[Dong] Xiwelcomed him at Gaoqian district [高遷亭].[Sun] Cemet him, and he regarded him as mighty. When they arrived [in the commandery], he was made provisionalAnti Bandit Officer under the Gates. At the time, the longstanding rebels ofShanyin[山陰],Huanglong LuoandZhou Bogathered thousands of their comrades.Sun Cemarched out to suppress them, and[Dong] Xipersonally beheaded[Huanglong] Luoand[Zhou] Bo. He then returned to be conferred asMajor of a Separate Unitand awarded thousands of troops. He was promoted toCommandant who Displays Martial Might. He followedSun Ceto attackWan[皖], suppressedLiu XunatXunyang[尋陽], and attackedHuang ZuatJiangxia[江夏].
When[Sun] Cedied,[Sun] Quanwas young in age, and when he first managed affairs, theConsort Dowagerworried about him and had him meetZhang Zhao,[Dong] Xi, and some others, asking if theJiangdong region[江東] could be secured or not.[Dong] Xireplied, “The geographical situation of theJiangdong region[江東] is that it has the defenses of hills and rivers. TheAdministrator who Exterminates Rebelsspread his grace and kindness to be with the people, and theGeneral who Suppresses Caitiffsinherited this foundation and has employed both the senior and the junior, withZhang Zhaobeing put in charge of domestic affairs, and I and the others as claws and teeth.2This is a time where the land is in prosperity and the people are in harmony. There is nothing among the myriad of matters that you need to worry about.” The masses all admired hisspeech.•••
This is a metaphor. “Claws and teeth” [爪牙] refer to military generals.↩
Peng Hu, a rebel ofPoyang[鄱陽], among others, had an army that numbered in the tens of thousands.[Dong] Xi,Ling Tong,Bu Zhi, andJiang Qineach separated with their own units and suppressed them. Wherever[Dong] Xiwent, he would always defeat [the foe]. When[Peng] Huand the others saw his flags and banners, they quickly scattered and fled. Within ten or so days, they were all pacified. He was conferred asColonel who Shows Might over the Yueand promoted toDeputy General.
In the thirteenth year of theera [208],[Sun] QuansuppressedHuang Zu.[Huang] Zuplaced two Mengchong ships3between the mouth of theMian[river] [沔] to defend it and used large ropes made of palm fibers attached to large stones that were made anchors.4There were one thousand people on top, and they used their crossbows to shoot, the flying arrows coming down like rain. The army could not advance.[Dong] XiandLing Tongboth were in the front units, and they each led one hundred Death Darer troops, and the people wore two layers of armor. They rode on a large ship and suddenly entered the interior of the Mengchong ships.5[Dong] Xipersonally cut down the two ropes, causing the Mengchong ships to drift out. The royal army thus advanced.[Huang] Zuquickly shut the gates and fled, but the army pursued him and beheaded him. The next day, there was a large assembly.[Sun] Quanraised his cup to toast[Dong] Xiand said, “The occasion today is for the achievement of severing therope!”•••
These were offensive ships covered with leather that could go at a high speed with its many oars. People could shoot out of the windows of the ship.↩
To clarify, the ships were placed at the edges of the body of water with an anchor on land that made them stay in their place. The ships could intercept any vessel that attempted to go through between them.↩
The adverb “suddenly” [突] implies that the Wu warriors caught them unawares, probably becauseHuang Zudid not expect the Wu army to advance.↩
WhenExcellency Caomarched toRuxu[濡須],[Dong] Xifollowed[Sun] Quanthere.[Dong] Xiwas instructed to supervise five Louchuan ships6to the mouth ofRuxu[濡須]. There was suddenly a violent wind during the night and the five Louchuan ships were turning over. His attendants all scattered and fled to smaller boats, begging[Dong] Xito leave.[Dong] Xiangrily said, “I received the appointment of General! We are here to defend against our enemies! How could I just leave? Whoever dares speak of this again shall be beheaded!” Hence, no one dared oppose him. That night, the ships capsized and[Dong] Xidied.[Sun] Quanchanged his own clothes [into mourning ones] and attended the funeral, giving very generoussupplies.7•••
These were ships with multiple decks stacked on top of each other. The decks had parapets so that people could shoot out of them, as well as having cover from enemy arrows.↩
Despite the biography not explicitly stating thatDong Xihad any relatives, the implication of this sentence is thatSun Quangave a lot of resources to his family so that they could maintain themselves.↩
